zh.lifeSummary: Isabel Bacon是Robert Bacon和Isabella d’Aviliers的女兒，出生在英國諾福爾可巴根斯索普。她與Oliver Calthorpe在英國英格蘭諾福爾可索普結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1411年在英國諾福爾可伯納姆 索普，埋葬在英國諾福爾可考可索普。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Isabel Bacon是Robert Bacon和Isabella d’Aviliers的女儿，出生在英国諾福爾可巴根斯索普。她与Oliver Calthorpe在英国英格兰諾福爾可索普结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1411年在英国諾福爾可伯納姆 索普，埋葬在英国諾福爾可考可索普。

nameMeanings.name: Bacon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from the Norman French personal name Bacun, derived from the ancient Germanic name Bac(c)o, Bahho, based on the element bag ‘(to) fight, (to) dispute’. The name was relatively common among the Normans in the form Bacus, of which the oblique case was Bacon.

English and French: from Middle English, Old French bacun, bacon ‘bacon’ (a word of ancient Germanic origin, akin to Back 3), probably a metonymic occupational name for a preparer and seller of cured pork.

History: Gilles Bacon from Normandy, France, is documented in Quebec City, QC, in 1647. — Michael Bacon from England arrived in Dedham, MA, in 1640. Nathanial Bacon, from Stratton, Cornwall, arrived in Barnstaple, MA, in 1639. Another Nathaniel Bacon (1647–76), from Friston Hall, Suffolk, emigrated to VA and settled at Curl's Neck on the James river.

variants.name: Bagg
meanings: from Middle English bagge ‘bag, pack, bundle’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of bags and sacks of various kinds, including wallets and purses. It might also have been used for a beggar, or for one who carried their belongings in a bag.
from the ancient Germanic personal name Bago (Bac(c)o, Bahho), from the root bag- ‘to fight’ (see Bacon 2).

variants.name: Back
meanings: English (Devon, Kent, Sussex, and Norfolk): from the Middle English personal name Bakke (Old English Bacca). It is of uncertain origin, but may have been a byname in the same sense as 3.
English: nickname from Middle English bakke ‘bat’, of uncertain application, perhaps a nickname for a person with poor eyesight, from the expression ‘blind as a bat’.

English: from Middle English bakke ‘back’ (Old English bæc), hence a nickname for someone with a hunched back or some other noticeable peculiarity of the back or spine, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or ridge, or at the rear of a settlement.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Isabel Bacon was born in Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, England as the daughter of Robert Bacon and Isabella d’Aviliers. She married Oliver Calthorpe in Thorpe, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1411, in Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk, England, and was buried in Cockthorpe, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Isabel Bacon est née à Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, fille de Robert Bacon et Isabella d’Aviliers. Elle a épousé Oliver Calthorpe à Thorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1411, à Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Cockthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Isabel Bacon wurde in Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, England, als Tochter von Robert Bacon und Isabella d’Aviliers geboren. Sie hat in Thorpe, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom Oliver Calthorpe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1411 in Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk, England und wurde in Cockthorpe, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Isabel Bacon è nata il nel Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, England come figlia di Robert Bacon e Isabella d’Aviliers. Lei sposato Oliver Calthorpe nel Thorpe, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1411, nel Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk, England, e fu sepolta a Cockthorpe, Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel BaconはイギリスノーフォルクバコンスソルペでRobert BaconとIsabella d’Aviliersの娘として生まれた。イギリスイギリスノーフォルクソルペでOliver Calthorpeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1411年にイギリスノーフォルクブルンハム ソルペで死去し，イギリスノーフォルクコクソルペにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Bacon(은)는 영국 노르폴크 바콘스소에서 Robert Bacon(와)과 Isabella d’Aviliers의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 소에서 Oliver Calthorpe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1411년 영국 노르폴크 번함 소에서 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 콕소에 묻혔다.

mn.Bacon Thorpe, Norfolk, England: Thorpe, Norðfolc, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Isabel Bacon nasceu em Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra filha de Robert Bacon e Isabella d’Aviliers. Ela casou-se com Oliver Calthorpe em Thorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1411, em Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em Cockthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Isabel Bacon родилась в местности бакунстаурп, норфолк, Англия. Её родителей звали Robert Bacon и Isabella d’Aviliers. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Oliver Calthorpe в местности таурп, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1411 г. в местности бурнам таурп, норфолк, Англия и была похоронена в местности кокктаурп, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Isabel Bacon nació en Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra como hija de Robert Bacon y Isabella d’Aviliers. Se casó con Oliver Calthorpe en Thorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1411, en Burnham Thorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, y fue sepultada en Cockthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
